context.predecessor: omega-interp-2 (94cd2b41) — push dirty-flag + high-water mark, ~10us/op interpreted
context.audio_3_baseline: vocal_hexa chain ~1037ms (hexa-native), warm python worker ~287ms (3.6x faster via numpy SIMD)
context.hypothesis: indexed assign on pre-allocated buffer would skip per-element call_builtin + env_set, dropping dispatch overhead 2-4x on N-bounded fixed-size buffers (e.g., vocal_hexa N=4800 bytes)

primitive_added.name: array_alloc
primitive_added.semantics: Returns array of n int slots, all zero. n<=0 yields empty array. O(n) once.

dispatch_arm.impl: tight while-loop pushing val_int(0) into a host items array, then val_array(items)

c_impl.summary: single malloc(N*sizeof(HexaVal)), fill with hexa_int(0), set len=cap=n (positive=heap). Mirrors hexa_array_zeros_float for ints. Currently AOT-only (interp dispatch arm uses hexa-side pushes; wiring AOT call site is deferred).

T4_4800_storage_micro.interpretation: alloc variant 6% SLOWER on pure-storage micro. Dispatch arm uses hexa-side pushes to build the zero-filled items array — pays N pushes upfront then N indexed assigns = 2N work vs N pushes. Indexed-assign per element ~= push per element after omega-interp-2 dirty-flag landed.

T4_storage_paired.config: N=4844 outer, 2 stores per i (mimics vocal_hexa low+high byte), R=50
T4_storage_paired.note: alloc 14% SLOWER — same root cause as T4 micro

vocal_hexa_bench.config: 440Hz, gain=0.5, 100ms (n=2400 samples, 4844 byte buffer), R=5 warm runs each
vocal_hexa_bench.interpretation: Effectively tied. Storage was never the bottleneck for vocal_hexa — sin/cos/floor + arithmetic per sample dominates. Optimizing the storage path alone leaves the chain ~unchanged.
vocal_hexa_bench.alloc_variant_added: vocal_hexa_alloc in /Users/ghost/core/anima/anima-speak/anima_audio.hexa (preserves vocal_hexa unchanged for A/B)

estimated_chain_speedup.verdict: HYPOTHESIS NOT BORNE OUT — array_alloc + indexed assign does not beat push-with-dirty-flag for vocal_hexa workload

root_cause_of_no_win.1_dispatch_overhead: host-interp dispatch arm for array_alloc uses N hexa-source `result.push(zero)` calls — these compile to N hexa_array_push C calls in the host interp binary. Equivalent to N pushes already, before user even starts indexed assigns.
root_cause_of_no_win.2_indexed_assign_parity: after omega-interp-2 dirty-flag landed (94cd2b41), per-element push ~= per-element indexed assign at the C-runtime level. Both are O(1) writes to the items buffer with one defensive array_store rebind.
root_cause_of_no_win.3_compute_dominates: vocal_hexa per-sample work is dominated by sin/cos/floor + arithmetic (10+ ops), not the 2 byte stores. Optimizing 2 stores out of 12 ops yields negligible delta.
root_cause_of_no_win.fix_path_for_real_win: wire host-interp call_builtin('array_alloc') to invoke the C primitive directly via a host_native bridge (similar to how `time_ms` calls C `time_ms()`). Single C call replaces N hexa-source pushes — would give true O(n) calloc-speed allocation. Requires extending the hexa_full.hexa builtin-table to include a host-native indirection for this name.

build_artifacts.runtime_c_changed_lines: +22 (hexa_array_alloc C function — AOT path; not yet wired)
build_artifacts.anima_audio_changed_lines: +82 (vocal_hexa_alloc variant for A/B bench, vocal_hexa untouched)

recommendation_for_audio_4.extend_to_other_organs: DEFERRED. Without a C-bridged dispatch for array_alloc, extending breath_hexa/pipe_hexa/vocal_lf_hexa to use the same pattern would yield 0-2% deltas (parity with push). NOT worth the surface-area churn.
recommendation_for_audio_4.alternative_path_1: First land a host-native bridge for array_alloc (call_builtin -> direct hexa_array_alloc C call). Re-bench vocal_hexa. If alloc drops to <50ms (chain <360ms vs current 1037ms), THEN proceed with audio-4 consumer migration.
recommendation_for_audio_4.alternative_path_2: Bottleneck is sin/cos compute. Consider sin_table[N] precompute or batch SIMD-style primitive (e.g., sin_array(t_array, hz, sr) -> y_array). This would attack the actual hot path.
recommendation_for_audio_4.alternative_path_3: Accept the 3.6x gap vs python worker as fundamental for now; optimize only the call sites where worker spawn overhead exceeds the synthesis cost.

rollback.risk: LOW. array_alloc is additive only — no existing call sites use it. vocal_hexa untouched (vocal_hexa_alloc is parallel sibling). C function is unreachable from interp path. Rollback is purely cosmetic.

outcome: primitive LANDED + selftest PASS, but NO speedup achieved on vocal_hexa. Chain stays at ~1037ms. ω-interp-3 is a NULL-RESULT cycle: hypothesis tested, falsified, root cause diagnosed (dispatch arm uses hexa-source pushes, defeating the calloc advantage). Path forward: host-native bridge before any audio-4 consumer migration.
